title: Fix --reapply data-loss: preserve ledger + skip meta regen when rules exist
description: F17: init --reapply silently truncates events.jsonl (init.ts:579-580 writeFileSync('')) AND regenerates agents.meta.json as L0-only stub (init.ts:572-573 + createInitialMeta L1256-1271), wiping AI-built rule node tree. Per D14: preserve events.jsonl byte-identically; skip agents.meta.json regen if rules already exist (detect via .fabric/rules/*.md presence). No new --reset command (D14, D34).
scope: Layer 2 P0 — silent data loss bug fix

files.change: Lines 579-580: remove unconditional events.jsonl truncation; preserve existing file. Lines 572-573 + createInitialMeta call site: detect existing .fabric/rules/*.md; if present, SKIP agents.meta.json regen (preserve AI-built tree); only regen if rules dir empty/missing.
Add ledger event reapply_completed { preserved_ledger: bool, preserved_meta: bool, rules_count }
NEW: tests asserting (a) events.jsonl byte-identical after --reapply; (b) agents.meta.json untouched when rules/ has content; (c) agents.meta.json regenerated when rules/ empty

criteria: agents.meta.json preserved when rules/ has .md files
agents.meta.json regenerated when rules/ is empty
reapply_completed ledger event written with preservation flags
Existing init flow tests still pass
